references.cue: Calmer, more consistent interface with dimmer sidebars and easier scanning across workflows.
references.adapted: Kept the task shell dense but quiet, with the rapid-entry tray feeling docked rather than modal.
references.rejected: Did not copy Linear's issue-detail density because the clinical shell needs more overt trust and freeze framing.

references.cue: Resizable sidebar, consistent tabs, and a full-focus main work area.
references.adapted: Used a restrained tab cycle in the dock so more-info and endpoint reasoning stay inside the same task shell.
references.rejected: Did not introduce another global navigation layer because the workspace route family already owns that responsibility.

references.cue: Icon-button action discipline, overflow handling at narrow widths, and explicit draft saving guidance.
references.adapted: Used compact chip-plus-text controls, responsive tray collapse, and explicit local-only autosave language.
references.rejected: Did not build a formatting toolbar metaphor because the workspace needs operational reasoning controls, not document editing chrome.

references.cue: Visible labels, grouped controls with legends, and errors tied clearly to their fields.
references.adapted: Kept every compose control explicitly labeled and used visible freeze explanations instead of color-only status.
references.rejected: Did not hide labels behind placeholder-only patterns.

references.cue: Be clear why each question is being asked and keep one dominant question at a time.
references.adapted: Question-set and more-info prompts are written as bounded, explicit asks tied to the current cycle.
references.rejected: Did not turn the reasoning layer into a wizard or multipage questionnaire.
